title: Indoctrination Theory Shinji Ikari
wikitext:
[]: Indoctrination Theory: Shinji Ikari             Quote Post by LordThaeon » Fri Jun 07, 2013 2:06 am Anyone care to humor me on this theory? Yes, I play a lot of Mass Effect before anyone asks. But it had me thinking... Shinji had to put with a lot of crap in his native series. Manipulative parents, peers and friends who belittled and used him as a means of escaping their own problems, hypocritical authority figures who demanded him to do what they themselves can't do, enigmatic monsters that pried into his very mind and soul, and to top it off with it being in a series that makes no sense from the get-go...I've come to respect Shinji pretty highly despite wanting him to do more in defiance of all of these forces that pull him here and there. Why isn't he standing up for himself? Because he's already doing so at a subconscious level. "Organics undergoing indoctrination may complain of headaches and buzzing or ringing in their ears. As time passes, they have feelings of "being watched" and hallucinations of "ghostly" presences." http://wiki.evageeks.org/images/f/f8/01_C022_Rei_III_girl_in_street.jpg Remember when he saw Rei just before he went to Nerv during the first episode? And then she was gone the next second? Under an Indoctrination Theory, Shinji's indoctrination picked up pace when he came to Nerv and in extension came within closer proximity with his indoctrinator. Who is the indoctrinator? Since we've seen evidence of angelic beings in the show being able to pry into someone's mind and soul and actively influence them as we saw with Shinji's encounter with Leliel, possibly Eva 03's possession by Bardiel, Arael's encounter with Asuka and Rei's encounter with Armisael, it is very possible that this extends to all other beings related to or created from the ancestors Adam and Lillith. So whose the Indoctrinator? It could be any of the following: Gendo through channeling Adam's power; Lilith underneath Nerv HQ and in extension, Tokyo 3; The Angels, specifically Kawaoru/Tabris; Rei, as the embodiment of Lilith's soul; or the evangelions themselves, specifically Unit 01 containing the soul of Yui Ikari. http://wiki.evageeks.org/images/c/c2/25_Shinji_Eva_01_Monstrous.jpg There's even evidence of possible indoctrination for others. Despite mistrusting Gendo's agenda and finding out about all of the lies in Nerv and their plans, Misato remains unyielding in having Shinji pilot the evangelion. Even though Gendo is clearly a bastard, she doesn't relent in using cold tactics and hypocritical posturing to get Shinji to pilot the Eva. She even kisses him under the belief that it will encourage him to go out and save Asuka, but ends up doing far worse. That's because she was likely indoctrinated by Adam during the Katsuragi Expedition and slowly influenced for the next fifteen years. Culminating in her being a reluctant, but stalwart agent of Gendo who eventually swallows Adam himself. Notice how she is less and less confrontational with Gendo as the series goes on, she never even questions him on his relationship with Shinji or call him out on his ruthless actions like using the dummy plug to force Unit 01 to destroy Unit 03 and maim/kill Toji... Speaking of Unit 03. Remember what Saren said in Mass Effect 1? "The more control Sovereign asserts, the less capable the thrall becomes. As Sovereign needs me, my mind is still my own, for now." The Indoctrinator will assert less control over more capable thralls, because then they can still use their own skills and abilities for the indoctrinator's benefit. Notice how Unit 03 was able to take down Unit 02 and Unit 00 with efficient tactics and expert use of an AT field? I think it even used it to hover at one point and then drop onto Unit 00. It also prevented Toji from ejecting, like it was actively using it as a hostage against it's fellow pilots. That doesn't sound like someone being used like a puppet. Under this theory, I believe that Unit 03 was indoctrinated to the Angel's cause by Bardiel in exchange for some form of revenge against it's creators/captors/jailers and it agreed. We already know that evangelions hate humans or at least loath them. Unit 00 went berserk twice and tried to kill anyone behind that glass panel; Unit 01 shows no empathy for nerv HQ as Zeruel is ripping it apart piece by piece and only activates when Shinji decides to return; Unit 02 is probably the least hateful towards humanity, but it isn't afraid of showing it's own pilot her place. The Angels themselves are probably being indoctrinated by Lilith herself. Why? To lure them into their deaths. They're all trying to go underground with the planted belief that they will unite with Adam, not Lilith their father's usurper. Remember Tabris' reaction when he came to Lilith and found it that it wasn't Adam? At that point, he basically gave up because he realized that he had been tricked and Adam was now beyond his reach thanks to a betrayed and distraught Shinji. That doesn't make them any less evil of course, but it does give an air of malice to Lilith if this is true. Here, we'll stick with Unit 01 as the primary indoctrinator since it truly does have the strongest evidence to back it up. Not to mention it has had a longer time to influence Shinji that stretches back to when he was three years old. http://wiki.evageeks.org/images/2/27/Toddler_shinji.JPG In all that time, Shinji has been battling this indoctrination without even realizing it. His will is fighting off the attempt subconsciously while Shinji goes about his life. That's because of Shinji's timid nature which is brought upon by a terrible life and never having anyone who encouraged Shinji to actively draw out on his inner strength. This strength has been forced to stay hidden or be destroyed by poisonous influences. It is this strength that allowed Shinji to last as long as he did amidst the multiple indoctrinators who pulled at his mind for his submission. Shinji's will is like an overworked slave, it is constantly pressured on all sides by both friend and foe, causing it to demand more of Shinji's strength to stay intact because without it, Shinji will more quickly submit to the Will of the Indoctrinators. As a result, Shinji as we see from the outside is a timid, insecure, lonely, depressed and scared boy whose doing what others say in an attempt to gain the love and acknowledgement that has been eluding him since he was three years old. Shinji is stronger than he thinks, but even the strongest mind will crack and break under too much pressure. http://wiki.evageeks.org/images/0/0e/25_Shinji_Chair_Plead.jpg First off, we have the train scenes that feature Shinji on a train talking to someone across the aisle. Sometimes it looks like Rei and other times like someone else. These scenes are all in Shinji's mind. They're Shinji's subconscious trying to get him to actively fight for himself and help against the battle for indoctrination because the pressure is increasing. Sadly, this is something that Shinji himself can't really help. His father has manipulated him into a situation that will damage him either way. If he pilots Eva, Eva and his subsequent battles will eat away at him while he hopelessly seeks his father's attention. When his hope spots are dashed away, Shinji will be left empty and alone, shouldered with a responsibility that he didn't ask for and burdened by a father who only sees him as a tool. If he had refused, then he would have been turning his back on humanity and all of the blame would have fallen on him for refusing. Even when he starts having doubts about piloting Eva, everyone turns on him. Even Misato is heartlessly cold towards Shinji even though she was initially reluctant about having him pilot. Each time he tries to leave, someone is there to call him a coward or tell him to suck it up and struggle through for humanity's sake. http://wiki.evageeks.org/images/a/a7/19_C067_shinji-stern.jpg Either way, Shinji is sacrificing his own well-being for minimal gain. Which in turn weakens his will's ability to resist Indoctrination. Especially when the primary source is coming from the very thing that he's being forced to pilot. "A Reaper's "suggestions" can manipulate victims into betraying friends, trusting enemies, or viewing the Reaper itself with superstitious awe...Long-term physical effects of the manipulation are unsustainable. Slow, patient indoctrination allows the thrall to last for months or years." http://wiki.evageeks.org/images/8/8e/Eva2-22_C0725_yui.jpg We all know that Yui chose to enter Evangelion and become it's inhabitant soul. Which means that she'd determined for Shinji to one day pilot the Eva since he was three years old. It's reasonable to believe that the indoctrination process had started then which left Shinji's will to fight an unseen war within his own mind...one where he was quickly losing ground as his life continued to get worse and worse. Yui, portrayed at first as a pure, loving mother, is actively using Shinji's maternal bond with her and the Evangelion's own abilities to indoctrinate Shinji as a means of achieving her variation of Third Impact. To do that, Shinji's willpower must be drained to achieve an inception of the notion that he'll never gain happiness. That no matter what, the human condition will deny him that which he so desperately desires and he's better off letting humanity turn into orange juice indefinitely because he'll always be alone. This will also cause him to associate Eva into more of a positive and maternal light despite it's nature as a parasitic monster in mecha form. As Shinji starts equating Eva with his mother, the last person to ever show him any semblance of unconditional love, the pull of indoctrination increases it's hold over him. Two events that further increase this are episodes that happen just as Shinji is starting to overcome his timid nature. Before Leliel, Shinji was becoming confident due to getting the highest synch-ratio, it was actually one of the few times when he was assertive and showed shades of badassery desplayed in Gurren Lagann. But afterwards, he regresses a little and never shows this show of bold and unadultered confidence again. That's because Yui allowed Shinji to be defeated by Leliel and didn't intervene until he was absolutely on the verge of death in order to plant into his mind that he can't resist on his own two feet. The same thing happened in the Zeruel fight. Shinji made a choice to pilot Eva of his own free will without any strings attached and managed to do pretty well against the angel. But then the power to his eva ran out and Zeruel was free to hammer into Unit 01. As it was torn at, Shinji is reduced from a defiant and pissed warrior to a crying and desperate child begging the eva to help him. So it does and completely takes over both the battle and Shinji's own form. While inside the Eva, it uses reverse psychology against him. Telling him that he won't be happy and then saying that the reason he isn't happy is because he isn't trying hard enough. Then after Nerv's attempt to return him fails, Yui gently tells him to return which he does. This was a ploy to further weaken Shinji's will further and took away any notion that he could ever be independent of his mother. Even his interactions with the other main characters are downers. Misato is a hypocritical mess who fails as a big sister and more often discourages Shinji than she does encourage him; Asuka is a selfish spoiled brat who often belittles and bullies him to where he starts to accept it (stockholm syndrome indeed); Rei physically punishes him for daring to point out that Gendo's a bastard; and Gendo...need I say more? All of these things and other events like the deaths of any positive influences in his life (Toji in the manga, Kaji, and to an extent Kawaoru/Tabris and Misato), the increasing toll that piloting the Evangelion has on Shinji, the battles that he's encountered, and so many unresolved desires and issues leave Shinji as a complete wreck by the time End of Evangelion comes around. With the deaths of Asuka and Misato, Shinji's willpower completely gives way and Eva 01 waits until Asuka dies to get Shinji and initiate Third Impact with the Eva Series. It seems like everything is going according to Yui's plan right? After all, Giant Naked Rei appears and seals the deal even further by turning everyone into orange juice, sucking them into a big black ball and taking Unit 01 into it's forehead... Wrong. Within the instrumentality of mankind, Shinji decides that this whole state of being is wrong. Despite all odds, his fragmented mind converses with him through the personas of people that he knows in order to get him to wake up. And against all probability, he succeeds and rejects instrumentality which in turn kills Giant Naked Rei along with all of the ancestrial beings and related species and allows humanity a chance to start over on their own two feet. Sadly, damage from Indoctrination is virtually permanent. http://wiki.evageeks.org/images/c/c9/M26_C469_Wideeyed_Shinji.jpg After fighting for twelve years and against insurmountable odds, Shinji is left utterly shell-shocked and barely has his mental faculties in check. I'm surprised that he even has those or that he isn't a complete suicidal mess. Further cementing his victory over indoctrination, he even stops choking Asuka once he realizes that she's actually there and then sobs at what he did. http://wiki.evageeks.org/images/5/5c/M26_Cheek_Touch.jpg With all things considered in this series, Shinji still held on to hope and is awarded hope in return. Now this theory only takes into account the original series and I have no idea if it would apply for the Rebuild movies or the manga. In fact, it could all just be a long string of coincidences. It was still fun to think up though.

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Fri Jun 07, 2013 10:36 am Somehow, I can get behind this even if it's completely insane.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Jun 07, 2013 11:14 am I'm deeply skeptical that Shinji is subject to any mind manipulation or control during NGE. While there is evidence that a younger Shinji has been put through some brainwashing in Rebuild, there's nothing to suggest this in the original series. But my biggest problem with this theory is that it cheapens and even makes meaningless Shinji very real struggles with depression. His ultimate, if Pyhrric, triumph over his own demons in EoE and especially EoTV is undermined completely by the idea they they were simply artificial to begin with. Shinji's personal difficulties and struggles are entirely conventional in nature, and are one of the elements that make Evangelion so compelling.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Sat Jun 08, 2013 6:36 pm Uhhh so why is this on Discussion sections instead of Chit-Chat? You can't possibly believe some wacky mind-control shenanigans (only vaguely skimmed the OP) is serious discussion material.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Jun 08, 2013 7:44 pm Dream wrote: Uhhh so why is this on Discussion sections instead of Chit-Chat? You can't possibly believe some wacky mind-control shenanigans (only vaguely skimmed the OP) is serious discussion material.

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Sun Jun 09, 2013 2:47 am It seems to have something to do with Mass Effect (haven't played it, don't know anything about it), so maybe it's kinda like crossover analysis?

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sun Jun 09, 2013 12:12 pm I could see Evangelion being a lengthy and agonizing deprogramming process that's only happening in Shinji's head ala The Prisoner if one A) disregards EoE entirely and B) assumes that Shinji is actually Gendo.

[]:             Quote Post by xdiesp » Mon Jun 10, 2013 7:13 am I don't think that there is a proper ME indoctrination mechanism in EVA, but different uses of the word instinct can cover that. Like animal instinct, which becomes the path designed by God for humanity or something. And death drive, or other drives, as mental instincts used in our design to achieve some fate or whatever. I can tell you this about the prince of the lilim . Giant mecha inventors leave their creations to their sons, to make them grow up: doc Kabuto in the very first mecha ever left his son the Mazinger, with some really deviant message about choosing what to make of this giant weapon of mass destruction - savior or demon. And once you have the responsibility, what would a prince do.

His ultimate, if Pyhrric, triumph over his own demons in EoE and especially EoTV is undermined completely by the idea they they were simply artificial to begin with. Shinji

 makes meaningless Shinji very real struggles with depression. His ultimate, if Pyhrric, triumph over his own demons in EoE and especially EoTV is undermined completely by the idea they they were simply artificial to begin with. Shinji's personal difficulties and struggles are entirely conventional in nature, and are one of the elements that make Evangelion so compelling.
